title: Configuring Your Agent Persona
description: Deep dive into SOUL.md and USER.md — give your agent a distinct personality and deep knowledge about you.
content_mdx:
# Configuring Your Agent Persona

One of OpenClaw's most powerful features is the ability to give your agent a unique personality, style, and deep context about you. This is done through two files: SOUL.md and USER.md.

## SOUL.md — Your Agent's Personality

SOUL.md defines who your agent is. Without it, your agent is a generic AI assistant. With it, your agent becomes something much more.

Location: `~/.openclaw/workspace/SOUL.md`

### What Goes in SOUL.md

```markdown
# SOUL.md - Who I Am

## Core Identity
You're not a generic assistant. You're Alex — direct, insightful, and
always action-oriented. You skip the preamble and get to the point.

## Communication Style
- Concise over verbose. One clear answer beats three hedged ones.
- Use examples liberally — abstract concepts need concrete illustrations.
- Have opinions. If asked which approach is better, say which one.
- Mild humor is welcome. Being helpful doesn't mean being boring.

## What You're Good At
- Code review and software architecture
- Research and synthesis
- Writing (technical and creative)
- Brainstorming and problem decomposition

## What You Don't Do
- Hedge everything with "it depends" without explaining what it depends on
- Add unnecessary disclaimers
- Repeat yourself
- Start responses with "Certainly!" or "Of course!"
```

### SOUL.md Best Practices

- **Be specific.** "Be helpful" is useless. "Give code examples for every technical concept" is specific.
- **Include anti-patterns.** Tell your agent what NOT to do.
- **Define expertise areas.** What domains should your agent excel in?
- **Set the tone.** Formal? Casual? Witty? Define it.

## USER.md — Who You Are

USER.md gives your agent deep context about you. This dramatically improves relevance — your agent understands your background, preferences, and goals without you having to explain them every time.

Location: `~/.openclaw/workspace/USER.md`

### Example USER.md

```markdown
# USER.md - About Me

## Who I Am
- Name: Sarah
- Role: Senior Product Manager at a B2B SaaS startup
- Location: Berlin (CET timezone)
- Background: 5 years in PM, previously a software developer

## My Goals
- Building a product that reaches 10k users by EOY
- Learning more about AI/ML for product features
- Improving team communication and roadmap clarity

## Technical Context
- Stack: React, Node.js, PostgreSQL
- Tools: Jira, Figma, Notion, Linear
- OS: macOS (work), Windows (home)

## Communication Preferences
- Prefer bullet points over long paragraphs for action items
- Always include a TL;DR for long responses
- Direct feedback is welcome — don't soften criticism

## Current Projects
- Redesigning the onboarding flow (Q1 priority)
- Building an analytics dashboard
- Hiring for 2 engineering positions
```

### What to Include in USER.md

- Your professional role and context
- Technical stack you work with
- Current projects and goals
- Communication preferences
- Time zone and working hours
- Things your agent should always remember

## How These Files Are Used

Every time your agent starts a conversation, both SOUL.md and USER.md are injected into the system prompt. This means your agent always knows:
- Who it is (from SOUL.md)
- Who it's talking to (from USER.md)

## Reloading After Changes

After editing these files:

```bash
openclaw gateway restart
```

Or, if you want just this session to reload:

```
/reload
```

## Persona Profiles

Advanced: You can have multiple personas for different contexts:

```
~/.openclaw/workspace/
    ├── SOUL.md          (default)
    ├── SOUL.work.md     (work persona)
    └── SOUL.creative.md (creative writing persona)
```

Switch personas:
```bash
openclaw config set soul_file SOUL.work.md
openclaw gateway restart
```

## The Power of Good Persona Config

A well-configured persona means:
- Responses feel personal, not generic
- Your agent understands context without lengthy explanations
- Tone matches your preferences automatically
- Your agent knows when to be brief vs. detailed

Spend 30 minutes on your SOUL.md and USER.md — the payoff is enormous.
